title: 沉睡谋杀案
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\140-沉睡谋杀案-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
Gwendolyn is distressed by the realization that a woman was murdered eighteen years ago. While Jules attempts to comfort her, Miss Marple arrives at the manor and joins Gwendolyn, Mrs. Coleridge, and Jules. Miss Marple asserts that recent events unequivocally confirm that Helen Harley was murdered at a young age.

Miss Marple proposes that Helen's body is not in the basement, as Edith Pettigrew previously claimed, but is instead buried at the end of the terrace. She explains that the relocation of the garden steps to the end of the terrace provided a pretext for digging, which would avoid suspicion from the gardener. Miss Marple notes that Lily Kimball’s letter mentioned a Swiss girl named Leonie, who saw something suspicious from the first-floor children's room window—likely the digging of a pit—but Leonie never reported it to the police.

Jules reconstructs the crime, suggesting the killer strangled Helen in the hall, moved her to a bed, and waited for Kelvin Harley to pass out from drugged whiskey before taking the body to the terrace to bury it. This implies the killer remained lurking near the house all night. Miss Marple advises that they must not trust anyone's word and should instead rely on confirmed facts. Jules begins to re-examine the suspects, specifically questioning the testimony of Erskine. While Gwendolyn believes Erskine is ruled out, Jules suggests that his claim of a mutual parting from Helen might be a lie to hide a motive of obsessive love and potential threats.
